federation.note: OSS-integration target #10 — a STANDARD-PROTOCOL READ/import bridge from CalDAV (RFC 4791) iCalendar (RFC 5545) calendars + CardDAV (RFC 6352) vCard (RFC 6350) address books into a Solid pod as the suite's established RDF models: VEVENT → schema:Event (typed quads + n3.Writer, NO hand-built RDF) keeping the W3C RDF-iCal ical: vocab for fields schema.org lacks (RRULE carried RAW as ical:rrule, NOT expanded in phase 1; subject typed both schema:Event AND ical:Vevent); vCard → vcard:AddressBook via @jeswr/solid-task-model's ./contacts buildPerson (SolidOS-readable structured vcard:hasEmail/hasTelephone nodes, never hand-built triples). PURE MAPPERS (veventToEvent/vcardToContact) are fixture-tested with no network; a minimal in-house RFC 5545/6350 content-line parser (line unfolding + property/param parsing) keeps the committed dist self-contained under ignore-scripts=true with no inlined third-party parser. importCalendar/importAddressBook accept EITHER already-fetched text OR a davUrl routed through @jeswr/guarded-fetch (https-only, block private/loopback/metadata, DNS-pin, cap+timeout, NO auto-redirect). DAV Basic/Bearer credential turned into an Authorization header only — NEVER logged, NEVER in a URL, NEVER re-sent cross-origin. Imports land OWNER-ONLY (never auto-shares, never writes a broadening ACL — inherits the required owner-private container); stable per-UID slug for idempotent re-sync. Untrusted-input hardened: unparseable date drops that field, non-http(s) IRI dropped, malformed entry drops the bad field rather than aborting the import. READ/import-only phase 1; write-back + sync-collection/ctag incremental + RRULE expansion are follow-ups; live DAV creds = needs:user. GitHub-installable now (committed self-contained dist/, ignore-scripts=true); npm publish deferred.
